resources.title: Registered Resources
resources.subtitle: Manage raw files, URLs, and databases ingested into the system.
resources.empty: No resources yet — click "Add Resource" to register one.
resources.llmAccessOnTitle: On — this resource's artifacts are exposed to LLMs via MCP. Click to turn off.
resources.llmAccessOffTitle: Off — hidden from LLMs via MCP (pipeline processing is unaffected). Click to turn on.

pipelines.title: Processing Pipelines
pipelines.subtitle: Define how specific resource types are routed through plugins.
pipelines.name: Name
pipelines.columnsCaption: Columns: plugin · max attempts · backoff (s) · timeout (s)

plugins.title: Plugin Registry
plugins.subtitle: Manage worker nodes that process resources into LLM artifacts.

community.panelSubtitle: A public directory of plugins shared by other DataCore operators — browse, then vet and deploy any you want to use yourself.
community.empty: No community plugins yet — be the first to post one.

communityLogin.subtitle: Your DataCore Community account is the same one used at the standalone community site — needed to share a bundle. No account is required to import one.
communityLogin.alreadyHaveAccount: Already have an account? Log in

shareBundle.title: Share as a DataCore Bundle
shareBundle.success: Bundle shared to the Community Registry.
shareBundle.noPipelines: You don't have any pipelines yet — create one before sharing a bundle.
shareBundle.explanation: Sharing exports your entire local pipeline list and their resource definitions (name/type/source) — not the processed artifact content. Anyone who imports it re-fetches sources and reprocesses through their own pipelines.
shareBundle.noEligibleResources: No eligible resources yet — a resource must be Completed, registered by URL (not uploaded), and of a type one of your pipelines above covers.

importBundle.title: Import from Community
importBundle.checkResourcesView: Check the Resources view — they'll process automatically if a matching pipeline exists.
importBundle.alreadyHavePipeline: You already have a local pipeline for: {{types}} — matching resources will start processing right away.
importBundle.missingPipeline: You don't have a pipeline for {{type}} yet ("{{name}}"). Resources of this type will still be registered and will sit Pending until you create one with these plugins:
